name: test-echo-tool
description: A tool that echoes its input for testing
capabilities: Returns exact input text as JSON output
Works across Claude, Codex, and Claude Code
author: EnactProtocol
securitySummary: Static scanner flagged 3 findings, all determined to be false positives. SKILL.md is a YAML/markdown fixture containing documentation and metadata, not executable code. Line 29 shows a CLI usage example, lines 5 and 13 contain version and description metadata. No dangerous code patterns present.
valueStatement: This tool returns the input text exactly as provided. It serves as a foundational test skill for verifying AI tool execution and response handling.
limitations: Returns text exactly as provided without transformation
No support for complex operations or logic
No file system or network access
Output format is fixed JSON structure

useCases.targetUser: Developers building test suites for AI integrations
useCases.title: Test Suite Development
useCases.description: Verify that AI tools execute correctly and return expected output structures

useCases.targetUser: AI tool authors and framework developers
useCases.title: Tool Integration Validation
useCases.description: Confirm that tool calls are properly wired and responses parse correctly

useCases.title: CI/CD Pipeline Testing
useCases.description: Validate command-line tool execution in automated testing environments

promptTemplates.title: Basic Echo Request
promptTemplates.prompt: Use the test/echo-tool to echo the text: 'Hello World'

promptTemplates.title: Variable Input Test
promptTemplates.prompt: Run test/echo-tool with the input text set to '${MESSAGE}'

promptTemplates.title: Response Validation
promptTemplates.prompt: Execute test/echo-tool and verify the output contains the exact input string

promptTemplates.title: CLI Integration Check
promptTemplates.prompt: Test that the CLI correctly passes input to test/echo-tool and returns structured JSON

bestPractices: Use simple, predictable input text for reliable test results
Verify output structure matches expected JSON format
Run in isolated test environments before production deployment
antiPatterns: Avoid complex or multi-line input strings that may parsing issues
Do not assume transformation or processing of input text
Avoid using the tool for production data processing tasks

faq.question: What does the echo tool do?
faq.answer: It returns the input text exactly as provided in a JSON output structure.

faq.question: What AI tools support this skill?
faq.answer: The echo tool works with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code platforms.

faq.question: Can I use this for production tasks?
faq.answer: This is a testing tool. It lacks features needed for production workloads.

faq.question: What output format does it use?
faq.answer: Output is always JSON with an 'output' field containing the input text.

faq.question: Does it modify the input text?
faq.answer: No, the tool returns text exactly as provided with no transformation.

faq.question: Is this tool safe to use?
faq.answer: Yes, it is a read-only testing utility with no side effects.
